whyExists: Procedural programming with loose variables gets chaotic in large systems. Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) allows you to bundle state (attributes) and behavior (methods) into reusable blueprints called Classes. Instances are actual objects constructed from that blueprint.
curiosityQuestion: What is the mandatory `self` parameter in Python class methods?
problemItSolves: Encapsulates data and behavior together into structured domain entities.
realWorldAnalogy: A Class is an architectural blueprint for a house. An Instance is a physical house built from that blueprint. You can build 50 houses (instances) from 1 blueprint (class).
simpleExplanation:
- Define class with `class Car:`.
- `__init__(self, ...)` constructor initializes instance attributes.
- `self` refers to the specific instance currently being acted upon.

examples.title: Class Blueprint & Instance Creation
examples.explanation: Defines BankAccount class with state (`owner`, `balance`) and method (`deposit`).

visualDiagram:
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    BP["Class Blueprint: User"] --> Ins1["Instance 1: Alice"]
    BP --> Ins2["Instance 2: Bob"]
```
commonMistakes: Forgetting `self` as first parameter in instance methods.
